title: Radar Sub-System Manufacturing
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 1 — European radar manufacturer establishing Saudi manufacturing operations
executive_summary:
Radar sub-system manufacturing — producing antenna structures, waveguide assemblies, power supply units, and signal processing chassis for military radar systems — is Phase II's most technically ambitious electronics manufacturing goal. Unlike communications equipment maintenance (DE-3) which is a service node, DE-4 is a manufacturing node: Saudi engineers and technicians physically building radar hardware to OEM specifications.

The opportunity is anchored by Saudi Arabia's position as one of the world's largest military radar customers — F-15SA APG-82 radars, Typhoon CAPTOR-E, ground-based air defence radars, and naval search radars represent billions of dollars of radar hardware deployed in-Kingdom. Non-ITAR European radar components (antenna structures, power electronics, signal processing boards for European-origin systems) are the Phase II manufacturing target.

The global military radar market was valued at $15.8 billion in 2024 and growing at 5.2% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's addressable Phase II manufacturing opportunity — structural and power sub-assemblies for European-origin radar systems — is estimated at $80–140 million over the Phase II period.

companies.name: Hensoldt
companies.rationale: Hensoldt manufactures military radar for Eurofighter and other European platforms. Hensoldt has active Saudi Arabia engagement and is exploring Gulf manufacturing investment.

companies.name: Thales Ground Radar
companies.rationale: Thales produces ground-based air defence and naval radar and has Saudi Arabia government relationships. French government IPP policy supports manufacturing localisation.
companies.contact: Thales VP Radar Systems, Industrial Cooperation

companies.name: Indra
companies.rationale: Indra manufactures naval and air defence radar and has Navantia programme relationships in Saudi Arabia. Non-ITAR Spanish technology.
companies.contact: Indra VP Electronic Systems, Saudi Arabia

companies.name: Leonardo Radar
companies.rationale: Leonardo manufactures radar antennas and structural sub-assemblies for military systems. Active Gulf defence market engagement.
companies.contact: Leonardo VP Radar and Electronic Warfare, International

companies.name: Saab Defence Electronics
companies.rationale: Saab produces radar systems for multiple platforms and has Middle East defence relationships. Non-ITAR Swedish technology.

partners.name: SAMI Electronics / Advanced Manufacturing
partners.current: Electronics assembly and maintenance; some defence electronics manufacturing
partners.upgrade: Hensoldt or Thales manufacturing JV; precision sheet metal and antenna structure fabrication; RF-grade electronics assembly; ISO 9001 and AS9100 for radar sub-assemblies
partners.timeline: 36 months to first manufactured radar sub-assembly

partners.name: KACST / National Electronics Technology Centre
partners.current: RF engineering research; microwave component design; some radar research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST radar technology programme; Saudi RF component research; support Hensoldt/Thales technology transfer to SAMI manufacturing

partners.name: Saudi Aramco / Industrial Technology Park
partners.current: Precision manufacturing; composite structures; some electronics assembly
partners.upgrade: Extend precision manufacturing to RF-grade radar antenna structures; dual-use (commercial + defence) precision fabrication

partners.name: KFUPM Electrical Engineering
partners.current: RF and microwave engineering; antenna theory research
partners.upgrade: Radar manufacturing engineering programme; KFUPM-industry placement at SAMI radar manufacturing facility; antenna design and manufacturing research
